channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: The $11k World Cup final ticket - what we learned from first open sale
item.description: The first open sale of tickets for the 2026 World Cup showed Fifa is charging up to $10,990 (£8,333) to be at the final.

item.title: Isak to return to training this week - Slot
item.description: British record signing Alexander Isak will return to Liverpool training on Thursday, head coach Arne Slot confirms.

item.title: Officials correct not to give Amad penalty - panel
item.description: The Premier League's KMI Panel says both referee and VAR were right not to give a penalty to Manchester United's Amad Diallo at Bournemouth.

item.title: Who uses academy players most in Premier League - and does it matter?
item.description: As Max Dowman becomes the latest academy product to make an impact in the Premier League, BBC Sport looks at who uses their youngsters most.

item.title: Italian FA head and Buffon quit over World Cup failure
item.description: Gabriele Gravina resigns as head of Italy's football federation after they fail to qualify for a third World Cup in a row, while ex-goalkeeper Gianluigi Buffon quits his role with the national team.

item.title: Kerr's uncertain future - should Chelsea keep striker?
item.description: The future of Chelsea striker Sam Kerr remains uncertain - but would it be a wise decision to let her leave on a free transfer this summer?

item.title: The remarkable rise of Tuchel's English assistant
item.description: Ten years after playing for Accrington, Anthony Barry is hoping to help Thomas Tuchel win the World Cup for England.

item.title: What is happening with Man Utd's teenage striker Chido?
item.description: Teenage striker Chido Obi made eight first-team appearances for Manchester United last season. This term, nothing. So how is his development going?

item.title: Davies back in Wales squad but Clark misses out
item.description: Mayzee Davies returns to the Wales squad for this month's World Cup qualifiers against Albania but there is no place for injured goalkeeper Olivia Clark.

item.title: Bompastor angry with VAR after McCabe hair pull
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor criticises VAR as Arsenal's Katie McCabe avoids a red card for pulling Alyssa Thompson's hair.

item.title: Is success crucial? BBC Sport experts pick their player of the season
item.description: With award season just around the corner, BBC Sport experts pick their contenders for Premier League player of the year. Do you agree?

item.title: The first promotion, Boro-Millwall showdown & Hodgson's back - EFL preview
item.description: Here are five things to look out for in the English Football League on Good Friday.

item.title: Arsenal reach Champions League semis as Chelsea's Bompastor sent off
item.description: Arsenal lose 1-0 in the second leg of their Women's Champions League quarter-final against Chelsea but win 3-2 on aggregate to reach the semi-finals and move a step closer to successfully defending their title.

item.title: McCabe avoids red after hair pull on Thompson
item.description: Watch the moment Katie McCabe appears to pull Alyssa Thompson's hair during Chelsea's 1-0 win against Arsenal in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final.

item.title: 'My players don't deserve this' - Bompastor furious after hair pull
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor says her players "deserve better" after she was sent off during her side's 1-0 win against Arsenal in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final, in a match which saw Katie McCabe appear to pull the hair of Chelsea's Alyssa Thompson.

item.title: Bayern strike late to knock Manchester United out of Champions League
item.description: Manchester United go out of the Women's Champions League as Bayern Munich score two late goals in the second leg of their quarter-final to win 2-1 on the night and 5-3 on aggregate.

item.title: 'You can't be doing that' - pundits react to McCabe hair pull
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Ellen White and Steph Houghton react to Katie McCabe's hair pull on Alyssa Thompson as Chelsea beat Arsenal 1-0 in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final, but lose the tie 3-2 on aggregate.

item.title: Game 'killer' or 'fairer' - does the Championship need VAR?
item.description: BBC Sport examines the arguments for and against and the thoughts across the Championship over the contentious issues of whether to bring in VAR.

item.title: Australia & Japan equal prize money request ignored
item.description: Asian Cup finalists Australia and Japan say organisers ignored their request to address the prize money inequality between men's and women's teams.

item.title: Is Scottish Premiership title race set for fresh twist?
item.description: After a two-week break from the carnage, what's next in one of the most gripping title fights in Europe?

item.title: Everton interested in Stones return - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Everton are monitoring John Stones' contract situation at Manchester City, Sandro Tonali's representatives contact Real Madrid, Barcelona lead the race to sign Bernardo Silva, plus more.

item.title: Wales boss Wilkinson condemns 'imbecile behaviour'
item.description: Wales head coach Rhian Wilkinson describes abuse on social media as "imbecile behaviour" after Wrexham's Adran Premier women's title success was followed by a string of negative comments.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Just a lesson for us, asks Askou after Slattery ban
item.description: Callum Slattery's four-game ban for simulation will only lead to a change in culture if consistency is applied, argues Motherwell manager Jens Berthel Askou.

item.title: How Arsenal are building something 'special' in Europe
item.description: Arsenal had their fairytale ending in Europe last season - but can they produce something magical again?

item.title: Chelsea make pre-tax loss of £262.4m - biggest in Premier League history
item.description: Chelsea announce a Premier League‑record pre‑tax loss of £262m for the 2024‑25 season.

item.title: BBC Sport's McNulty picks his England World Cup squad - do you agree?
item.description: BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty picks his 26-man England World Cup squad - do you agree?

item.title: Captain Donnarumma vows to restore Italy after heartbreak
item.description: Italy captain Gianluigi Donnarumma says he "cried because of the enormous sadness" of not being able to lead the national side to the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Little wants more Champions League success as Arsenal reach final four
item.description: Arsenal captain Kim Little says she believes her side are "getting better and better" as they reach the semi-finals of the Women's Champions League, after beating Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate to close in on successfully defending their title.

item.title: I can't expect to jump in at top level - Defoe
item.description: Former England striker Jermain Defoe hopes he can secure promotion with National League side Woking as the 43-year-old replaces Neal Ardley to take on his first managerial role.

item.title: Watch: Scotland lose friendly against Ivory Coast
item.description: Watch the best of the action as Scotland are beaten 1-0 by Ivory Coast at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium.

item.title: Highlights: Wales draw 1-1 with Northern Ireland
item.description: Watch highlights as Wales draw 1-1 with Northern Ireland in their international friendly match in Cardiff.

item.title: Wiegman on 'speechless' Parkinson's England call-up
item.description: England manager Sarina Wiegman explains why she has handed a first senior call-up to 17-year-old midfielder Erica Meg Parkinson for World Cup qualifiers with Spain and Iceland.

item.title: From YouTube to real life: why Hashtag United want to go down
item.description: After a decade of moving up the football pyramid, YouTube team Hashtag United want to be demoted. BBC Sport asks co-founder Spencer Owen: Why?

item.title: Foden not guaranteed World Cup squad spot - Tuchel
item.description: Manchester City midfielder Phil Foden's is not guaranteed a place in England's squad for the 2026 World Cup, says manager Thomas Tuchel.

item.title: The World Cup line-up is complete - here's what you need to know
item.description: The final six nations have qualified for the 2026 World Cup - this is your essential lowdown on the expanded edition of the tournament.

item.title: Man Utd are closing in on Champions League - how has Carrick done it?
item.description: Manchester United are within touching distance of a return to the Champions League. How has interim boss Michael Carrick done it?

item.title: Lamine Yamal condemns racist abuse aimed at Egypt
item.description: Lamine Yamal says racist chants heard during Spain's 0-0 draw with Egypt in Barcelona on Tuesday were "disrespectful and intolerable".

item.title: Defoe ready to 'earn his stripes' with Woking
item.description: Jermain Defoe hopes he can be a trailblazer for black managers as he targets promotion with National League side Woking.

item.title: Is Roberto de Zerbi the man to save Spurs?
item.description: Mark Chapman, Steph Houghton and Ashley Williams discuss Roberto de Zerbi's prospects, with the Italian in advanced talks with relegation-threatened Tottenham.

item.title: 'Japan beat England too, so let's be real' - Robertson on Scotland defeats
item.description: Captain Andy Robertson discusses the reaction of a section of Scotland fans after the friendly defeats by Japan and Ivory Coast.

item.title: Tudor's five Premier League games as Spurs boss
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look back at Igor Tudor's five Premier League games in charge as Tottenham Hotspur boss.

item.title: Chelsea leave it late to win seven-goal thriller over Villa
item.description: Chelsea secure a vital three points as they beat Aston Villa 4-3 to go second in the league table and get back on track for Champions League qualification.

item.title: Brighton condemn Leicester City to seventh-straight league loss
item.description: A lone goal from Brighton's Kiko Seike is enough to condemn Leicester City to a seventh-straight loss in the WSL while Albion move up to sixth.

item.title: Siren scores screamer as West Ham draw at London City
item.description: Oona Siren's stunning volley helps Women's Super League relegation battlers West Ham fight back to earn a point against London City Lionesses.

item.title: Watch: Brown's last Ayr game as Championship leaders also stumble
item.description: Watch our round-up of highlights from Scotland's lower leagues as Scott Brown's Ayr United reign ends in defeat, Championship leaders St Johnstone drop home points, Stenhousemuir miss a chance to top the third tier, while Spartans and East Kilbride draw at the top of League 2.

item.title: Scotland's World Cup build-up begins with narrow Japan loss
item.description: Watch the best of the action from Hampden as Junya Ito's 84th-minute strike consigns Scotland to defeat in their first World Cup warm-up friendly.

item.title: Russo scores hat-trick as Arsenal beat Spurs
item.description: Striker Alessia Russo nets a first-half hat-trick as Arsenal beat north London rivals Tottenham 5-2 in front of a crowd of more than 46,000 fans at Emirates Stadium.

item.title: Man City edge closer to WSL title by beating Man Utd
item.description: Vivianne Miedema's double helps Manchester City edge closer to the Women's Super League title with a comfortable derby win over neighbours United.

item.title: 10-player Liverpool survive fightback to win Merseyside derby
item.description: Ten-player Liverpool hold on for their first Women's Super League away win of the season with a hard-fought victory over local rivals Everton at Goodison Park.

item.title: Wales' World Cup dream ends in pain of penalty shootout
item.description: Wales' hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup are ended by Bosnia-Herzegovina in Cardiff as the away side score a late equaliser before winning on penalties in their play-off semi-final.

item.title: Chelsea lead Premier League's record £460m spend on agent fees
item.description: Premier League clubs made payments of £460m to agents over the past year - a 13% increase on the previous 12 months.

item.title: Who were Scotland's winners and losers from international break?
item.description: After a friendly defeats by Japan and Ivory Coast, which Scotland players are the winners and losers of the international break?

item.title: Man arrested on suspicion of racially abusing Sunderland's Geertruida
item.description: A Gateshead man is arrested on suspicion of racially abusing Sunderland's Lutsharel Geertruida during last month's Premier League match at Newcastle.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup quarter-finals on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Lewandowski misses out on World Cup - who else won't be there?
item.description: The World Cup includes 48 nations, but there are still a number of top players who have missed out on qualification.

item.title: Seven games to save Tottenham's season - De Zerbi becomes Spurs boss
item.description: Roberto de Zerbi is appointed Tottenham's third boss of the season - with a clear objective for the next two months.

item.title: Ranking the 10 most exciting wide men in the world
item.description: Few things in football are more exciting than an in-form wide man, but who are the most exciting in the world right night?

item.title: How Sky can ignore limit on its TV picks & why fans are fed up
item.description: Premier League games being moved to Friday or Monday are a headache for the fans of some clubs. Is the system fair, and how does it work for Sky?

item.title: How do Man City replace captain Silva if he leaves?
item.description: Manchester City could find themselves without the services of their manager and captain next season with the futures of both Pep Guardiola and Bernardo Silva in question beyond this summer.

item.title: The remarkable return of Roy Hodgson aged 78
item.description: Roy Hodgson's idea of celebrating 50 years in management is not to sit back reflecting on his career, but instead to return to the dugout at Bristol City.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How does Tudor's record compare to other Tottenham bosses?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team compare Igor Tudor's managerial record to other Tottenham managers of recent years.

item.title: Why are Scotland playing Ivory Coast in Liverpool?
item.description: Scotland continue their World Cup preparations with a friendly against Ivory Coast on Tuesday, but why is this away game being played at Everton's new stadium in Liverpool?

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: Kilmarnock's Watson being watched by Bologna - gossip
item.description: Reported Serie A interest in Kilmarnock midfielder as Rangers are linked with an international goalkeeper...

item.title: Lang's surgery for thumb injury 'went well'
item.description: Galatasaray midfielder Noa Lang is set to have surgery in Liverpool after suffering a "serious cut" at Anfield.

item.title: Who are the record Champions League goalscorers?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team looks at which players have scored the most goals in the Champions League.

item.title: Why ex-Palace winger Olise is now in Ballon d'Or contention at Bayern
item.description: Michael Olise has been in superb form for Bayern Munich this season, has the French winger put himself in contention for the Ballon d'Or?

item.title: Laporta re-elected Barcelona president for fourth term
item.description: Joan Laporta vows Barcelona will be "unstoppable" as he is re-elected club president for a further five years.

item.title: From Bodo to Brentford - ranking this season's biggest overachievers
item.description: Bodo/Glimt must be the most romantic story in European football in decades but are they the team that has defied the odds the most this season? Here are all 10 contenders...

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
